news.headline: US labor market surpasses expectations with 256,000 nonfarm payrolls in December, unemployment rate at 4.1%. Wall Street erases post-election gains amid interest rate concerns.
news.summary: This week, Wall Street erased its post-election gains as concerns over rising interest rates intensified due to a strong labor market, high inflation expectations, and growing geopolitical tensions in anticipation of Donald Trump's return to the White House. The trading week was shortened due to the funeral of former President Jimmy Carter, but market activity peaked on Friday. The U.S. economy added an impressive 256,000 nonfarm payrolls in December, surpassing economists' projection of 160,000 jobs and marking the strongest job growth since March. The unemployment rate also fell to 4.1%, lower than the expected 4.2%. In 2024, the U.S. labor market ended the year with exceptional performance.

news.headline: Accolade (NASDAQ:ACCD) reports Q3 earnings with EPS of $-0.3, missing estimates by -87.0%.

news.headline: System1, Inc. notified by NYSE for non-compliance due to average closing share price < $1.00 over consecutive 30 days.
news.summary: In Los Angeles, System1, Inc. (NYSE: SST) disclosed that the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE) has raised concerns regarding its non-compliance with Section 802.01C of the NYSE Listed Company Manual. This was due to the average closing price of the Company’s Class A Common Stock falling below $1.00 for 30 consecutive trading days. The Company, known for its omnichannel customer acquisition marketing platform, received this notification. This development does not immediately affect the Company's listing status on the NYSE.

news.headline: Raytech Holding reported a 31.0% increase in revenue to HKD 43.2 million (US$ 5.6 million) for the six months ended September 30, 2024. However, net income fell by 27.5% to HKD 4.7 million (US$ 0.6 million) due to rising operating expenses. Company aims to optimize costs while boosting sales growth.
news.summary: Raytech Holding Limited, a Hong Kong company specializing in personal care electrical appliances, reported a 31.0% increase in revenue to HKD 43.2 million for the six months ended September 30, 2024, up from HKD 33.0 million the previous year. Operating expenses rose sharply, with merchandise costs increasing by 40.3% to HKD 34.1 million and selling, general, and administrative expenses climbing to HKD 5.2 million. Income from operations decreased by 43.7% to HKD 3.9 million, and net income fell by 27.5% to HKD 4.7 million. Despite this, the company remains committed to improving profitability and market position by optimizing cost structures and driving sales growth. For more information, visit Raytech Holding Limited's website.

news.headline: byNordic Acquisition Corporation extends business combination deadline, depositing $40,312 for an additional month until Feb 12, 2025.
news.summary: Nordic Acquisition Corporation (NASDAQ: BYNO) disclosed depositing $40,312 into the Trust Account to extend the deadline for completing a business merger for an extra month, now set from January 12, 2025, to February 12, 2025. This is the sixth extension out of a possible twelve allowed under the August 8, 2024 amendment to the Company’s Amended and Restated Certificate of Incorporation. The amendment empowers the board to extend the termination date by one month each time, with the last possible extension being until August 12, 2025, or until the completion of the initial merger.

news.headline: La-Z-Boy Inc. unveils FY2024 Impact Report focusing on sustainability & community efforts.
news.summary: A new report reveals that over 3,000 people in the city have been affected by a recent spike in a rare strain of flu. Health officials are urging residents to take precautionary measures to prevent further spread of the virus. The local hospital has seen a significant increase in patients with flu-like symptoms, with over 500 cases reported in the past week alone. Authorities are advising the public to get vaccinated and practice good hygiene to protect themselves and others from the illness.

news.headline: DecisionPoint Technologies (DPT) reveals new brand identity after merger; now North America's top enterprise mobility solution.

The rebranding signifies the company's transformation and emergence as the leading provider of enterprise mobility and supply chain solutions in North America. Drawing on their extensive experience and cutting-edge technology, DPT aims to enhance operational efficiency, positioned as a key player in this sector.

news.headline: Artisan Partners Asset Management's AUM as of Dec 31, 2024, totaled $161.2 billion, split into $77.6 billion from Artisan Funds and Artisan Global Funds, and $83.6 billion from separate accounts and other AUM.
news.summary: In Milwaukee, on January 10, 2025, Artisan Partners Asset Management Inc. (NYSE: APAM) announced that its preliminary assets under management ("AUM") as of December 31, 2024, were $161.2 billion. Of this total, Artisan Funds and Artisan Global Funds made up $77.6 billion, while separate accounts and other AUM stood at $83.6 billion. During December, certain Artisan Funds distributed their annual income and capital gains. Additionally, December month-end AUM encompassed the impact of roughly $450 million of Artisan Funds distributions that were not reinvested.

news.headline: US bank deposits fell to $17.748 tln from $17.865 tln in the prior week.
Swiss Helvetia Fund plans special meeting to discuss new investments; if approved, a 30% cash distribution likely.
news.summary: On January 10, 2025, The Swiss Helvetia Fund, Inc. (NYSE: SWZ) disclosed lodging preliminary proxy materials with the SEC for a forthcoming special stockholders' meeting to determine the Fund's potential investment advisory agreement with Bulldog Investors, LLP and modifications to its investment objective to broaden allowable investments. Pending stockholder approval, the Board of Directors plans to liquidate a significant portion of the Fund's portfolio assets and issue a unique cash payout – mainly comprising long-term capital gains – amounting to around 30% of the Fund's net assets.

news.headline: Montana Renewables closes $1.44B DOE loan for renewable fuels/biomass energy facility; $782M to be funded this month; 300M gallons/year Sustainable Aviation Fuel capacity.
news.summary: A loan amount totaling $782 million will be received in stages, with an initial disbursement expected to be completed by the end of this month. The rest of the funds will follow through the construction phase. The loan guarantee from the Department of Energy is enabling the expansion of Sustainable Aviation Fuel production capacity to reach 300 million gallons annually. This development is set in Indianapolis and took place on January 10, 2025.
